 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","title":"EPIC: VPS Installer (install.sh)","description":"# Epic: VPS Installer (install.sh)

## Overview
Build the one-liner bash installer that transforms a fresh Ubuntu VPS into a fully-armed agentic coding environment.

## Background & Reasoning
This is the "magic moment" - the user pastes one command and everything happens. The installer must:
- Work when run as root (common VPS default)
- Work when run as ubuntu with sudo
- Be idempotent (safe to run twice)
- Handle connection drops and resume
- Log everything for debugging

## Design Philosophy
**Vibe Mode Default**: Passwordless sudo, agents with full permissions, minimal friction.
**Checkpointed Phases**: Each phase writes state so reruns skip completed work.
**Module Manifest**: Single source of truth (acfs.manifest.yaml) drives all installs.

## Installer Phases
1. Preflight + confirm OS (Ubuntu 24.04+ / 25.x)
2. Create/normalize `ubuntu` user + sudoers
3. Base packages (apt)
4. Shell (zsh + oh-my-zsh + p10k + plugins)
5. Dev toolchain (bun, uv, rust, go, tmux, rg, ast-grep)
6. Agent stack (claude/codex/gemini CLIs + aliases)
7. Flywheel stack (ntm, slb, ubs, cass, cm, bv, caam, mcp_agent_mail)
8. Post-install UX (onboard + doctor + summary)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Fresh Ubuntu 25.x VPS → working environment in < 15 minutes
- [ ] Safe to run twice without breaking anything
- [ ] All 8 Dicklesworthstone tools installed and verified
- [ ] `acfs doctor` shows all green
- [ ] Logs available at /var/log/acfs/install.log

## Considerations
- Always prefer upstream installers when available
- Verify each tool after install
- Provide clear error messages with fix suggestions
- Support `--dry-run` for transparency","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T22:21:06.097272-05:00","labels":["epic","installer"]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","title":"Create installer logging library (scripts/lib/logging.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Installer Logging Library

## Description
Build a reusable logging library for consistent installer output.

## Functions

```bash
log_step()    # [1/8] Step description (blue)
log_detail()  # Indented detail (gray)
log_success() # ✔ Success message (green)
log_warn()    # ⚠️ Warning message (yellow)
log_error()   # ✖ Error message (red)
log_debug()   # Only if ACFS_DEBUG=1
```

## Requirements
- All output to stderr (keep stdout clean)
- Support --quiet flag (suppress non-errors)
- Support --verbose flag (show debug)
- Timestamps in log file
- ANSI color codes with fallback

## Log File
Write all output to:
- `/var/log/acfs/install.log`
- `~/.acfs/logs/install.<timestamp>.log`

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/logging.sh

ACFS_LOG_FILE="/var/log/acfs/install.log"
ACFS_QUIET="${ACFS_QUIET:-0}"
ACFS_VERBOSE="${ACFS_VERBOSE:-0}"

log_step() {
    local step="$1"
    local msg="$2"
    echo -e "\033[34m[$step] $msg\033[0m" >&2
    echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] STEP: $msg" >> "$ACFS_LOG_FILE"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All log functions implemented
- [ ] Colors work in terminal
- [ ] --quiet suppresses output
- [ ] --verbose shows debug
- [ ] Log file written with timestamps
- [ ] Works when sourced by other scripts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.871116-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.34494-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:17.872789-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","title":"Create modern CLI tools installer (scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh)","description":"# Task: Create Modern CLI Tools Installer

## Description
Install modern CLI replacements that the zshrc depends on.

## Tools to Install

### Via apt (Ubuntu 24.04+)
- ripgrep (rg) - fast grep
- fd-find (fd) - fast find
- bat/batcat - better cat
- fzf - fuzzy finder
- tmux - terminal multiplexer
- neovim - better vim

### Via cargo (more recent versions)
- lsd - modern ls with icons
- dust - better du
- btop - better top (or via apt)
- zoxide - better cd
- ast-grep (sg) - structural grep

### Via curl/installer
- lazygit - git TUI
- lazydocker - docker TUI
- direnv - directory-specific env

## Functions
```bash
install_apt_cli_tools()
install_cargo_cli_tools()
install_other_cli_tools()
verify_cli_tools()
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tools installed
- [ ] Fallback logic (apt first, cargo if newer needed)
- [ ] Verification for each tool
- [ ] Idempotent","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.469899-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:27:23.299944-05:00","close_reason":"Created scripts/lib/cli_tools.sh with modular installer functions for apt, cargo, and curl-based CLI tools","labels":["installer","mvp","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.10","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:37:54.472277-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.12","title":"Create post-install smoke test","description":"# Task: Create Post-Install Smoke Test

## Description
Automatic verification that runs at the end of install.sh.

## What It Tests

### Critical Path (must pass)
1. User is ubuntu
2. Shell is zsh
3. Passwordless sudo works
4. /data/projects exists
5. bun, uv, cargo, go available
6. claude, codex, gemini commands exist
7. ntm command works
8. onboard command exists

### Non-Critical (warn only)
- Agent Mail server can start
- All 8 stack tools respond to --help
- PostgreSQL service running

## Output
```
[Smoke Test]
✅ User: ubuntu
✅ Shell: zsh
✅ Sudo: passwordless
✅ Workspace: /data/projects exists
✅ Languages: bun, uv, cargo, go
✅ Agents: claude, codex, gemini
✅ NTM: working
✅ Onboard: installed

⚠️ Agent Mail: not started (run 'am' to start)
⚠️ PostgreSQL: not running (optional)

Smoke test: 8/8 critical passed, 2 warnings
```

## Integration
- Called automatically at end of install.sh
- Exit 0 if all critical pass
- Exit 1 if any critical fail (with guidance)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All critical checks implemented
- [ ] Clear pass/fail output
- [ ] Warnings for non-critical
- [ ] Runs in < 10 seconds","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:39.577781-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T11:54:49.548513-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T11:54:49.548514-05:00","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.12","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:38:39.579953-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.2","title":"Create OS detection library (scripts/lib/os_detect.sh)","description":"# Task: Create OS Detection Library

## Description
Build a library to detect and validate the target OS.

## Requirements
- Detect Ubuntu version (24.04, 25.x)
- Detect architecture (amd64, arm64)
- Fail gracefully on unsupported OS
- Provide useful error messages

## Functions

```bash
detect_os()          # Returns: ubuntu
detect_version()     # Returns: 25.04
detect_codename()    # Returns: plucky
detect_arch()        # Returns: amd64 or arm64
validate_os()        # Exit 1 if unsupported
```

## Supported Configurations
- Ubuntu 24.04 LTS (noble)
- Ubuntu 25.04 (plucky)
- Ubuntu 25.10 (future)
- Architectures: amd64, arm64

## Implementation

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/lib/os_detect.sh

detect_os() {
    if [[ -f /etc/os-release ]]; then
        . /etc/os-release
        echo "$ID"
    else
        echo "unknown"
    fi
}

validate_os() {
    local os=$(detect_os)
    local version=$(detect_version)
    
    if [[ "$os" != "ubuntu" ]]; then
        log_error "ACFS requires Ubuntu. Detected: $os"
        exit 1
    fi
    
    # Check version >= 24.04
    if [[ "${version%.*}" -lt 24 ]]; then
        log_error "ACFS requires Ubuntu 24.04 or later. Detected: $version"
        exit 1
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correctly detects Ubuntu versions
- [ ] Detects arm64 vs amd64
- [ ] Clear error on unsupported OS
- [ ] Works on fresh Ubuntu VPS","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:30.433099-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346095-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346095-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:30.435198-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.3","title":"Create user normalization library (scripts/lib/user.sh)","description":"# Task: Create User Normalization Library

## Description
Build a library to ensure the ubuntu user exists with correct configuration.

## Requirements
- Create ubuntu user if missing
- Set up passwordless sudo
- Copy SSH authorized_keys from root
- Set zsh as default shell (after zsh installed)

## Functions

```bash
ensure_ubuntu_user()     # Create user if missing
setup_passwordless_sudo() # Write sudoers.d file
copy_ssh_keys()          # From root to ubuntu
set_default_shell()      # Change to zsh
```

## Sudoers Configuration
File: `/etc/sudoers.d/90-ubuntu-acfs`
```
ubuntu ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD:ALL
```

## SSH Key Handling
If running as root:
1. Check /root/.ssh/authorized_keys exists
2. Create /home/ubuntu/.ssh/ if needed
3. Copy authorized_keys
4. Fix permissions (700, 600)
5. Set ownership to ubuntu:ubuntu

## Groups
Add ubuntu to:
- sudo
- docker (after docker installed)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Creates ubuntu user if missing
- [ ] Passwordless sudo works
- [ ] SSH keys copied from root
- [ ] Correct file permissions
- [ ] Works when run as root or ubuntu
- [ ] Idempotent (safe to run twice)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:42.426684-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346616-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T23:23:59.346616-05:00","close_reason":"Files created in previous session: scripts/lib/logging.sh, os_detect.sh, user.sh, zsh.sh, and install.sh with all 8 phases","labels":["installer","task"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6.3","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-5m6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-19T22:24:42.428514-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
